title: Variables
beginnerExplanation: A variable is a readable name that points to a specific value Python has stored in memory. Think of it as a label attached to data so you can easily find and reuse it later.
intermediateExplanation: Without variables, we would have to type the raw data (like a player's exact score or name) every single time we needed it. Variables allow us to write flexible code that works no matter how the data changes during the game.

realWorldAnalogy.title: Moving Boxes
realWorldAnalogy.description: Imagine moving to a new house. You pack your books into a box and write "Books" on the outside. You don't need to look inside every box to find your books; you just look for the label. In Python, the variable name is the label, and the value is what's inside the box.

mentalModel: Imagine moving to a new house. You pack your books into a box and write "Books" on the outside. You don't need to look inside every box to find your books; you just look for the label. In Python, the variable name is the label, and the value is what's inside the box.

executionWalkthrough: 2. Scopes and local namespaces are verified.
3. Target variables are allocated or updated.
commonMistakes: Writing the value on the left side, like 10 = score.
Using spaces in a variable name, like player name.
Forgetting that variables are case-sensitive (Score is different from score).

debuggingMission.scenario: An adventurer is trying to use Variables but hit a common pitfall: Writing the value on the left side, like 10 = score.
debuggingMission.explanation: Make sure to follow the best practices: Use descriptive names like player_health instead of ph.

practiceQuestions.question: Which of the following is the correct way to assign a variable in Python?
practiceQuestions.explanation: In Python, the variable name must be on the left side, followed by a single equals sign, and then the value on the right.

practiceQuestions.question: What happens when you assign a new value to an existing variable name?
options: The old value is kept alongside the new one.
The variable label moves to point to the new value.
practiceQuestions.explanation: Variables can change! Assigning a new value simply points the label to the new data, forgetting the old one.

hints: Look at the syntax summary of the topic.

bestPractices: Use descriptive names like player_health instead of ph.
Make sure the name reflects what the data actually means.
